$statusLegend.ported: byte-equivalent to upstream (CI fails on drift)
$statusLegend.adapted: TypeScript port and/or namespace swap; logically equivalent
$statusLegend.extended: fork adds behavior on top of upstream
$statusLegend.added: file unique to the fork (no upstream)

files.notes: name + textdomain swapped to artisanpack namespace; attributes/supports/styles verbatim.
TypeScript port. Adds explicit 'wp-block-separator' class to useBlockProps so the editor canvas matches frontend independent of __experimentalSelector internals.
TypeScript port; class list adds 'wp-block-separator' explicitly (parity with edit).
Upstream input/raw transforms preserved (target the new namespace via metadata.name); added bidirectional block transforms for core/separator ↔ artisanpack/separator round-tripping.
Combined SCSS-to-CSS port. data-type selectors rewritten from core/separator to artisanpack/separator.
Inline SVG for the inserter (editor doesn't bundle dashicons).
Adapted to the visual-editor auto-discovery contract. Upstream's PHP-side init wiring and example settings are not carried.

knownDivergences: edit.tsx + save.tsx add 'wp-block-separator' to useBlockProps explicitly; upstream relies on __experimentalSelector to inject it.
transforms.ts adds bidirectional block transforms that don't exist upstream — required to coexist with core/separator during the V2 rollout.
index.ts replaces upstream's PHP-side init wiring with the visual-editor auto-discovery contract; the `example` settings object isn't carried.
